capabilities.adaptation: Python names the value GuardrailResult; Rust names it GuardrailOutcome.
Python returns a configured facade; Rust uses a builder.
Python calls the embedded session AgentSession; Rust calls it InteractiveSession.
The languages expose their native session error hierarchy.
Python names the sender facade OutgoingRouter; Rust names it OutgoingMessageSender.
Python adapts registry handlers; Rust adapts ToolSpec values.
Both errors report the same portable-regex contract using native naming.
Python exports a concrete event class; Rust exports a RunEventPayload variant.
Python exports a concrete event class; Rust exports a RunEventPayload variant.
The acronyms follow each language's naming convention.
Python exposes ScriptedLLM; Rust exposes ScriptedLlmClient.
Python defines the backend protocol; Rust uses a backend enum.
Python integrates Celery; Rust exposes the distributed backend used by Apalis adapters.

members.adaptation: Python stores the public list directly; Rust configures it through AgentBuilder.
Python stores the public list directly; Rust configures it through AgentBuilder.
Python stores the target type; Rust exposes its type name and validator.
Rust stores stop names inside the ToolUseBehavior enum variant.
Python exposes a synchronous class facade; Rust exposes an async instance method.
Python returns a synchronous iterator; Rust returns an async-capable RunEventStream.
Python takes optional input directly; Rust also exposes resume_with_input.
Python returns ConfiguredRunner; Rust returns RunnerBuilder.
Python stores seconds as a float; Rust stores Duration.
Python names arbitrary host state context; Rust names it app_state.
Both values seed per-run shared state using native naming.
Python groups trace controls in a mapping; Rust uses typed trace fields.
Python reads trace_id from the tracing mapping; Rust exposes a field.
Python reads workflow_name from the tracing mapping; Rust exposes a field.
Python accepts a callback; Rust exposes independent typed RunHandle event streams.
Python names the inner runtime result raw_result; Rust names its getter result.
Python executes synchronously; Rust awaits the session run.
Python creates synchronously; Rust resolves the session asynchronously.
Python owns a blocking loop; Rust awaits a generic transport server.

protocol_operations.adaptation: Both initialize facades send the initialized notification after the initialize response.

supporting_operations.adaptation: Python accepts a timeout; Rust uses its configured response timeout.

members.adaptation: The field follows each ecosystem's schema naming.
Python stores seconds; Rust stores Duration.
Python accepts bool/callable; Rust exposes ToolApprovalRule.
Rust exposes tool metadata through ToolExecutor.
Python returns the OpenAI schema directly; Rust returns ToolSpec containing it.
Rust executes through ToolExecutor after adapting Tool to ToolSpec.
Python exposes ordered names plus get_executor; Rust exposes ordered executor objects.
Python can register an executor directly; Rust registers its ToolSpec adaptation.
Python passes stream_callback to complete; Rust exposes a separate defaulted method.

verification.python: public export resolution plus dataclass field/property/getattr/callable/inspect.signature checks
verification.rust: compiled exports and exhaustive method/function references plus typed public-field accessors
